title: Introduction to Cybersecurity
subtitle: What cybersecurity means and why it matters to you

concepts.title: Cybersecurity is everyone's responsibility
concepts.body: Hackers don't just target big companies — they target individuals. Your email, bank account, social media, and even your device's camera are all potential entry points. Security isn't just an IT problem; it's a personal one.

concepts.title: The three pillars: CIA
concepts.body: Confidentiality — only the right people see the data. Integrity — the data hasn't been tampered with. Availability — the data is accessible when needed. Every security control you'll learn maps to one of these three.

concepts.title: Most attacks exploit human behavior
concepts.body: Technology is hard to break directly. So attackers trick people instead — they send convincing emails, create fake websites, or impersonate trusted contacts. The strongest defense is knowing how these tricks work.

quiz.prompt: Check your understanding of Cybersecurity basics.

questions.question: What does the 'C' in the CIA triad stand for?
questions.explanation: The CIA triad stands for Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability — the three core goals of cybersecurity.

questions.question: Why do attackers often target people instead of technology?
options: Technology is too fast to hack
People can be tricked more easily than systems can be broken
People have more money than computers
questions.explanation: Social engineering exploits human psychology. It's usually easier to trick a person than to find a technical vulnerability.

questions.question: Which scenario is an example of a confidentiality breach?
options: A website goes offline during a storm
An attacker reads your private emails without permission
A password is changed by the account owner
questions.explanation: Confidentiality means keeping data private. Someone reading your emails without authorization is a direct violation of confidentiality.

takeaways: Cybersecurity protects your data, money, and identity
The CIA triad — Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability — is the foundation
Attackers target people because human behavior is the weakest link

title: Real-World Case Studies
subtitle: Lessons from actual cybersecurity incidents

concepts.title: Ransomware targets financial infrastructure
concepts.body: The Bank of Zambia and Zanaco (Zambia National Commercial Bank) were hit by a Clop ransomware attack in November 2025. Operations were disrupted for days as systems were taken offline to contain the spread. Clop is known for exploiting file-transfer vulnerabilities.

concepts.title: Crypto exchanges are high-value targets
concepts.body: Coinbase suffered a security breach in early 2025 where attackers exploited a flaw in the email authentication workflow. Employees received convincing phishing calls that led to credential compromise, showing that even well-resourced platforms are vulnerable to social engineering.

concepts.title: Retail breaches expose millions
concepts.body: Marks & Spencer experienced a cybersecurity incident in April 2025 that disrupted online and in-store operations. The incident involved unauthorised access to customer accounts, potentially exposing personal data and loyalty information.

takeaways: Social engineering bypasses even strong technical controls
Ransomware can cripple critical national infrastructure
Security awareness is everyone's responsibility, not just IT
